channel.title: SaaStr Blog 日本語要約
channel.description: SaaStr Blogの記事を日本語に要約したフィード

item.title: The Agent Playbooks from Salesforce, Snowflake, Databricks, Harvey, and Lovable: Inside SaaStr AI 2026 (翻訳失敗)
item.description: The main focus of the SaaStr AI 2026 event in San Mateo was on the role of agents in SaaS businesses. The event featured sessions where speakers discussed how agents are actively involved in production, sales quotas, data management, and overall business operations, revolutionizing the way companies operate. The event highlighted various speakers and their titles, emphasizing the recurring themes that emerged throughout the sessions. These themes underscored the significance of agents in reshaping business models and strategies across different sectors. The event showcased how agents are becoming integral to the core operations of SaaS companies, impacting their growth and success. SaaS businesses can leverage this insight by incorporating agent-centric strategies into their operations to enhance efficiency, productivity, and overall performance. (翻訳失敗)

item.title: 「マンスリー・ブランド・エンジン」：モナコが30日ごとに新たなバズを生み出した方法
item.description: Sam Blond, CEO of Monaco and ex-CRO of Brex, discussed at SaaStr AI 2026 the importance of leveraging AI in go-to-market strategies to automate routine tasks, freeing up time for more impactful human-driven activities in SaaS businesses. AI excels at handling mechanical aspects of marketing, leaving room for human strengths like building relationships with customers and crafting creative campaigns, which are crucial for brand building. The key takeaway is that AI should not be a reason to reduce marketing efforts but an opportunity to focus on activities that AI cannot replicate, such as creative brand initiatives. Blond suggests implementing a strategy of creating one major brand moment every month to sustain brand momentum and engagement. These moments should not be isolated stunts but interconnected initiatives that reinforce each other, creating a cumulative impact on brand awareness and engagement. By consistently delivering these brand moments, SaaS companies can build a strong and lasting brand presence. (翻訳失敗)
